summary:
There is no consistently identified major pattern of accessory canals in mandibular first molars; instead, significant anatomical variability is recognized across individuals and tooth types. The most accurate clinical takeaway is that accessory canals exhibit considerable diversity without adhering to a single predominant configuration.  

$\boxed{\text{D}}$
